sourceEvidence.label: Housing Application Packet Builder readiness evidence

assessment.summary: One cross-cutting tenant request-boundary opportunity was validated and its incremental option implemented.

nonNegotiables: Preserve local Node/npm/SQLite development without Docker.
Keep production PostgreSQL row-level security as defense in depth.
Fail closed when organization scope is absent.
assumptions: No measured request-latency or memory budget was supplied.

opportunities.title: Make tenant request authority explicit
opportunities.summary: Ensure every authenticated page, action, and API route activates organization authority in its own async caller.
opportunities.diagnosis: The database correctly failed closed, but organization authority was previously entered inside an awaited helper and did not survive into the caller continuation.

evidence.claim: Authentication resolves and validates the session organization before tenant access.
Every tenant Prisma operation requires organization context and adds model scope.
The pre-fix caseworker dashboard failed closed because the caller had no organization context.
Ambient authority can drift when request boundaries do not activate context after authentication awaits.

desiredInvariants: Every authenticated request boundary activates exactly the organization validated by its live session before tenant database access.
Production database RLS and application-layer scope agree on the organization identifier.
New authenticated handlers cannot enter CI without the activation pattern.

options.title: Caller-bound activation with a static guard
options.summary: Activate context synchronously in each authenticated caller and enforce coverage in tests.

evidenceCoverage.rationale: The caller now owns activation and the browser workflow passes.
The CI guard detects omission, while authority remains ambient within the activated caller.

tradeoffs.assessment: The reproduced missing-context path now succeeds only after explicit activation, while absent context still fails closed.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Run tenant isolation, auth-boundary, and Playwright suites.

tradeoffs.assessment: Activation is one AsyncLocalStorage operation with no database or network hop.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Compare dashboard request latency if a performance budget is later established.

tradeoffs.assessment: The change stores one existing organization identifier in the request async context.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Observe process RSS during the existing browser suite.

tradeoffs.assessment: All six browser workflows pass and missing activation is caught before runtime by a source guard.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Keep the boundary guard and E2E workflow required in CI.

tradeoffs.assessment: No service is added; failures retain the existing safe workspace error behavior.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Alert on repeated portal render failures in deployed logs.

tradeoffs.assessment: Existing service APIs remain unchanged and current callers were mechanically migrated and type-checked.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Reject any unactivated authenticated caller in CI.

residualRisks: Ambient organization authority remains available to all tenant services called within an activated request.

workPackages: Add static boundary guard and browser regression coverage.
acceptanceCriteria: No authenticated app caller awaits authentication without activation.
All tenant and Playwright tests pass.
implementationReadiness.rollback: Revert caller changes only with a replacement organization-authority mechanism; otherwise authenticated tenant access fails closed.

options.title: Capability-scoped organization database client
options.summary: Pass an organization-bound database capability through services instead of ambient request context.

evidenceCoverage.rationale: Bound clients remove the missing ambient context, but caller activation remains necessary during migration.
Organization authority becomes a typed service dependency rather than a convention.

tradeoffs.assessment: Tenant authority is explicit and can be narrowed per service, reducing ambient-authority drift.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Prove no tenant service imports the global tenant client and rerun cross-organization tests.

tradeoffs.assessment: Prisma can share the underlying pool, but wrapper construction and parameter plumbing need measurement.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Compare p50 and p95 dashboard and packet-generation latency on identical PostgreSQL data.

tradeoffs.assessment: Bound wrappers should be lightweight if they share one client; accidental per-request pools would regress memory sharply.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Assert one underlying Prisma pool and compare peak RSS under concurrent requests.

tradeoffs.assessment: Typed authority removes async-context loss but a broad migration can introduce service wiring errors.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Migrate one vertical slice at a time behind compatibility tests.

tradeoffs.assessment: Runtime operations stay in-process; developer tracing improves because organization authority is visible in call signatures.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Review error telemetry during a staged dual-path rollout.

tradeoffs.assessment: Pages, actions, API routes, jobs, and application services must accept or construct the capability.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Inventory imports and require zero direct global tenant-client imports before cutover.

residualRisks: System-level clients remain privileged and must retain narrow database grants.
Incorrect capability construction can still select the wrong organization unless bound only from validated sessions or worker iteration.

acceptanceCriteria: Tenant services require an explicit bound capability.
No cross-organization read or write succeeds in SQLite or PostgreSQL.
migrationNotes: Use caller-bound activation until every service is migrated.
implementationReadiness.rollback: Retain the current activated-context path until the capability migration is complete and validated.

opportunities.recommendation: Use caller-bound activation for the current release; choose capability-scoped clients before adding plugins or independent application services.

openQuestions: At what service count or extension boundary should capability-scoped clients become mandatory?
